title: Geopolitiks Snacks — 31 mars 2026
content:
**Geopolitiks Snacks** — March 31, 2026

---

## 💣 Pentagon Preps Ground Troops for Iran — But Don't Worry, Trump Says "Diplomacy First"

Oh good, we're on Day 31 of bombing Iran AND now the Pentagon is "readying for weeks of ground operations." Totally different vibe from yesterday.

- Ground raids are reportedly being planned for **Kharg Island and coastal Strait of Hormuz sites** — the same oil infrastructure that's already cratered global energy markets
- Trump escalated his rhetoric, threatening to "blow up and completely obliterate" Iranian electric plants and oil wells if a deal isn't reached — standard peace negotiation tactics
- The **Houthis entered the chat** for the first time, firing drones that Israel intercepted, because apparently one war front wasn't enough flavor
- Pakistan is preparing to host "meaningful talks." Cool. The meaningful talks are happening while the meaningful bombs are also happening.

*Nothing says diplomacy like building up for ground ops while threatening to vaporize oil infrastructure. Perfectly calibrated.* 💀

**Sources:** [Al Jazeera Day 31](https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/3/30/us-israel-war-on-iran-whats-happening-on-day-31-of-attacks) | [CNN Day 31](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/30/world/live-news/iran-war-us-israel-trump)

---

## 🔴 US Launches *Yet Another* Review of Nvidia Chips to China (Groundhog Day Edition)

Shocking: the same chips we keep saying China can't have, China keeps getting. And we keep reviewing whether to tighten the rules. The cycle continues.

- The US Commerce Department opened a **fresh review of Nvidia's H20 and Blackwell chips** on March 30, possibly adding new licensing requirements
- Nvidia's Chinese market share has already slid from **90% to ~50%** as local competitors fill the gap — and we're going to make that faster
- Meanwhile, **Beihang University** (you know, the one doing aerospace and defense research) acquired a machine-learning workstation with four banned Nvidia A100 chips in March 2026. Export controls are *so* controlled.
- The House passed the **Chip Security Act** on March 26 to curb smuggling — presumably because the current honor system isn't working

*We keep writing the same press release about export controls while Nvidia chips keep appearing in Chinese military university server racks. Beautiful system.* 🚢

**Sources:** [TechCrunch: Sweeping New Chip Controls](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/05/us-reportedly-considering-sweeping-new-chip-export-controls/) | [Tom's Hardware: Banned Chip Acquisition](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/chinese-universities-performing-military-research-acquired-super-micro-servers-with-sanctioned-nvidia-ai-chips-public-documents-reveal-purchases-were-completed-in-2025-and-2026-despite-us-export-controls) | [ChinaTechScope](https://chinatechscope.com/nvidia-deepseek-ai-chip-war/)

---

## 🤖 China Just Rolled Its 10,000th Humanoid Robot Off the Line (We Are So Normal)

Who would've thought that the nation we're in a chip war with would be *also* winning the humanoid robot production race? Agibot hit **10,000 units** on March 30 — with the last 5,000 built in just three months.

- That's a **4x acceleration** in production speed. Going from slow to ludicrous speed. Tesla Optimus and Figure are watching nervously from their American garages.
- Agibot ranked **#1 globally in humanoid robot shipments** in 2025, and they're deployed across logistics, retail, and manufacturing in Europe, North America, Japan, and the Middle East
- The first 1,000 units took *nearly two years*. The last 5,000 took *90 days*. This is what scaling actually looks like.

*The chip export controls are definitely working great. Love the control we have over this situation.* 🦾

**Sources:** [Robotics and Automation News](https://roboticsandautomationnews.com/2026/03/30/agibot-reaches-10000-humanoid-units-built-as-real-world-demand-for-robots-accelerates/100218/) | [Interesting Engineering](https://interestingengineering.com/ai-robotics/china-agibot-10000th-humanoid-robots) | [The Information](https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/chinese-humanoid-maker-agibot-rolls-10-000th-mass-produced-unit)

---

## 🏛️ White House to States: Your AI Laws Are Illegal Now, Thanks

The Trump administration released its **National AI Legislative Framework** on March 20 and the vibes are very "we're killing your state laws, deal with it."

- The framework calls for **federal preemption** of state AI laws — meaning California's AI regulations, Texas's, all of them — gone, replaced by one "minimally burdensome" national standard
- It would also make AI developers **immune from liability** for third-party misuse of their models. In case you wanted to know who this is actually for.
- Both Democrats AND some Republicans are already pushing back — the **GUARDRAILS Act** was introduced specifically to block this. Nothing unites Washington like a really aggressive industry handout.
- The EU, meanwhile, is doing the opposite: extending regulation timelines but keeping the rules. The US and Europe have officially agreed on absolutely nothing about AI governance.

*"Minimize the burden on AI companies" is doing a lot of heavy lifting in a document nominally about protecting children.* 📋

**Sources:** [CNN: White House AI Framework](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/20/tech/white-house-ai-framework) | [WilmerHale Analysis](https://www.wilmerhale.com/en/insights/blogs/wilmerhale-privacy-and-cybersecurity-law/20260323-white-house-releases-national-policy-framework-for-artificial-intelligence) | [Freshfields: State Preemption](https://blog.freshfields.us/post/102mnul/white-house-publishes-ai-legislative-framework-to-preempt-state-ai-regulation)

---

## 📧 Google Is Now Reading All Your Free Users' Gmail — For AI. But It's Fine. It's Opt-In.

On March 17, Google removed the paywall from Gemini Personal Intelligence, which means Gemini now has access to your Gmail, Google Photos, YouTube history, and Search data — for every free US user. Totally normal product decision.

- Until last month, this was a **paid feature**. Now it's just... everyone.
- Google swears it's not training on your inbox directly — just on the prompts you send *about* your inbox. Different! Legally distinct! Probably!
- The rollout spans Google Search AI Mode, the Gemini app, and Chrome. So basically everywhere you already are.
- The FTC and state privacy regulators are "watching." Great. Looking forward to the 2029 investigation.

*"Your data helps improve the model" is doing more work than any AI agent right now.* 👁️

**Sources:** [Humai Blog: Gemini Personal Intelligence](https://www.humai.blog/google-rolls-out-personalized-gemini-to-all-us-users-ai-now-reads-your-gmail-and-photos/) | [RoboRhythms](https://www.roborhythms.com/google-gemini-personal-intelligence-free-gmail-march-2026/) | [Prism News](https://www.prismnews.com/news/google-opens-gemini-personal-intelligence-to-free-users-nationwide)

---

## 🛒 ChatGPT's Shopping Feature Flopped — So Now It's Pivoting to "Discovery"

Remember when OpenAI was going to replace Amazon with ChatGPT checkout? Yeah, about that. The **Instant Checkout** feature is dead, replaced by something much more humble: helping you *find* products you then buy somewhere else.

- Instant Checkout launched with ~30 Shopify merchants and was "prone to errors." For the love of God.
- OpenAI is now leaning into the **Agentic Commerce Protocol** — basically a fancier product search with visual comparisons and Walmart, Target, Sephora, Best Buy etc. already integrated
- Walmart still gets a shiny "in-ChatGPT app" but it links back to Walmart's own checkout. We've reinvented the shopping tab.

*OpenAI entered the e-commerce space and discovered that e-commerce is, in fact, a solved problem. Bold strategy.* 🛍️

**Sources:** [CNBC: Agentic Shopping Stumbled](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/20/open-ai-agentic-shopping-etsy-shopify-walmart-amazon.html) | [Digital Commerce 360](https://www.digitalcommerce360.com/2026/03/24/openai-agentic-commerce-updates-chatgpt-walmart/) | [OpenAI: Product Discovery](https://openai.com/index/powering-product-discovery-in-chatgpt/)

---

title: Geopolitiks Snacks — 30 mars 2026
content:
**Geopolitiks Snacks** — March 30, 2026

---

## 🔥 The U.S. and Israel Are Currently Bombing Iran (No Big Deal)

Oh great, we're doing *this* now. U.S. and Israeli forces have been striking Iran for going on three weeks, and things are, uh, not chill.

- U.S. strikes hit Kharg Island, which handles **90% of Iran's crude oil exports** — so naturally, Brent crude rocketed 47% to ~$116/barrel in under a month
- The Strait of Hormuz (responsible for 20% of the world's oil supply) is partially closed. You know, that thing that keeps global energy markets from melting down
- Trump reportedly threatened to "take the oil" and floated *seizing the island outright* because apparently escalation is the move
- Thousands killed; Pakistani diplomats are trying to broker talks while the rest of the world watches with their jaws on the floor

*Totally normal spring 2026 stuff. Gas prices are fine. Everything is fine.* 💀

**Sources:** [CNN Live Coverage](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/29/world/live-news/iran-war-us-israel-trump)

---

## ✊ "No Kings" Hits Different: 8 Million People in the Streets

Who would've thought that economic chaos + an ongoing war would make people slightly upset? Saturday's "No Kings" protests drew an estimated **8 million people** across all 50 U.S. states and more than a dozen countries — potentially the largest single-day protest in American history.

- Gas just hit $4.06/gallon, up 46 cents in two weeks (thanks, Iran war), and consumer confidence cratered to 53.3, its lowest of 2026
- Protests happened globally, not just domestically — it's giving "the world is watching" energy
- The name "No Kings" is doing a lot of work and people are feeling it

*Nothing unites a country like $4 gas and an optional war.* 🪧

**Sources:** [Democracy Now Headlines](https://www.democracynow.org/2026/3/30/headlines)

---

## 🤖 Physical Intelligence is Now Worth $11 Billion (Congratulations, I Guess)

Shocking that a company called "Physical Intelligence" would be valued at an amount that feels entirely unphysical. The ex-DeepMind robotics startup is reportedly in talks to raise ~$1 billion in new funding at an **$11 billion valuation**.

- Pi was founded by AI researchers who left Google DeepMind to go build robots that can do things in the real world (novel concept)
- $11B for a robotics startup that hasn't shipped at scale is *very* VC-brained but also somehow expected at this point
- Robotics capital is flowing like it's 2021 crypto, and everyone's betting the physical AI wave is real

*We're one funding round away from robot startups being worth more than small countries.* 💸

**Sources:** [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-27/ex-deepmind-staffers-robotics-startup-in-talks-for-11-billion-valuation)

---

## 🏦 SoftBank Borrows $40 Billion to Keep the AI Lights On

Ah yes, the classic "take out a massive bridge loan to fund your massive bets on AI" strategy. SoftBank arranged a **$40 billion bridge loan** running through March 2027, primarily to fund OpenAI investments and keep the whole operation afloat.

- Meanwhile in France, Mistral secured **$830 million in debt financing** just to buy 13,800 Nvidia chips and build a data center near Paris
- Big Tech — Google, Microsoft, Meta, Oracle, xAI, OpenAI, Amazon — are all pledging to build or buy new power generation *specifically for AI data centers*
- The AI infrastructure buildout is now requiring nation-scale capital and energy commitments. Cool cool cool.

*"We'll borrow our way to AGI" is genuinely the strategy and nobody is blinking.* 🏗️

**Sources:** [StyleTech Top News March 2026](https://www.styletech.net/post/top-news-in-tech-march-2026)

---

## 🔧 Elon Musk Announces $20B Chip Fab Because Sure, Why Not

Not content with owning a car company, a rocket company, an AI company, a social network, and a government efficiency role, Elon Musk announced **Terafab** — a joint $20+ billion semiconductor fabrication facility in Austin, Texas, run by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI together.

- The fab will produce custom chips for EVs, Optimus humanoid robots, and high-performance AI compute
- This is the "vertical integration but make it industrial policy" era: build your own chips, your own robots, your own everything
- America's chip independence narrative + one billionaire's empire-building goals somehow rhyme here

*It's fine. Everything about this is fine. One man owning the entire tech stack from chips to robots to space is completely normal.* ⚙️

**Sources:** [TechStartups March 25 Roundup](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/25/top-tech-news-today-march-25-2026/)

---

## 🦾 Northwestern Built Robots That Refuse to Die

In "news that's either awesome or terrifying depending on your mood," Northwestern University engineers just unveiled **"legged metamachines"** — modular robots that can self-repair, reconfigure on the fly, and keep working after being damaged.

- They're built from autonomous Lego-like modules that can be combined and recombined in the field
- Injured? The robot *evolves around the injury* and keeps going. Relatable, honestly.
- Northwestern calls this "athletic intelligence." The robots call it nothing because they can't talk. Yet.

*We built the Terminator but cute and academic about it.* 🏃

**Sources:** [Northwestern University News](https://news.northwestern.edu/stories/2026/03/evolved-robots-are-born-to-run-and-refuse-to-die)

---
